name: Candied Fruitcake
recipeInstructions:
Make no substitutions  Assemble ingredients and let stand at room temperature for 1 hour.
1. Set oven to 275 degrees.
2. Use 2 (9") springform pans or 2 (9x5) loaf pans; or use 1 of each. Grease the pans and line with brown paper cut to fit, then grease the paper.
3. Cut 3 (7 oz.) packages of pitted dates and 1 lb. candied pineapple into coarse pieces. Put in a large bowl. Add 1 lb. whole candied cherries.
4. Sift 2 cups of sifted flour, 2 teaspoons baking powder, and 1/2 teaspoon salt onto the fruit.
5. Mix fruits and dry ingredients well with your fingers, separating pieces so that all are well coated.
6. With an electric beater, beat 4 eggs until frothy.
7. Gradually add 1 cup of granulated sugar; beat until blended.
8. Add the fruit mixture, mixing well with a large spoon.
9. Add 2 lbs. (8 cups) pecan halves. Mix with your hands until nuts are evenly distributed and coated with batter.
10. Pack into pans, pressing down with fingers. If necessary, rearrange pieces of fruit and nuts to fill up any empty spaces.
11. Bake springform cakes about 1 hour and 15 minutes...loaf cakes 1 hour and 30 minutes. When done, tops of cakes should look dry. If there is any doubt, leave cakes in the oven a few minutes longer; as a little extra baking does no harm.
12. Remove from oven and let stand on cake racks about 5 minutes before turning out onto racks and carefully pulling off paper.
13. Cool cakes completely, then turn top side up.
